findings.title: Stripe Tax is off in production — first UK/EU web sale is sold VAT-exclusive with no VAT collected, locking in the wrong tax posture from transaction one
findings.detail: STRIPE_TAX_ENABLED defaults false (.env.example:103) and every item on the flip checklist is unchecked and Grace-owned (TODO.md:44-53; docs/operations/stripe-tax-launch-checklist.md status 2026-05-13 'dashboard config + Vercel flag pending', no later update). With the flag off: /pricing renders 'Price excludes any applicable taxes.' (app/pricing/PricingTiersGrid.tsx:496), resolveRenderedVatNote suppresses the VAT note entirely, and /api/stripe/checkout omits automatic_tax so Checkout charges the bare £7.99/£59.99 sticker with no VAT line. The code is honest and self-consistent — this is not a copy lie — but it directly violates the locked 2026-04-19 decision (UK/EU consumer VAT applies from £1/€1; prices must be VAT-inclusive; Stripe Tax inclusive mode). Every Stripe-channel UK/EU sale accrues VAT liability Suppr owes retroactively out of net revenue. The dedicated 'Stripe Tax mode != inclusive' alarm is also Not yet wired (docs/operations/alerting.md:172-175), so nothing forces this before the first charge. Caveat: live Vercel flag value inferred from repo artifacts, not read from the dashboard — but the fallback copy the flag controls is what ships, so the inference is strong.
findings.recommendation: Before the first web payment: Grace activates Stripe Tax in the dashboard, sets tax_behavior=inclusive on all 4 Price objects, flips STRIPE_TAX_ENABLED=true in Vercel, and runs the live UK checkout smoke test — the exact 4-step checklist already in TODO.md. Engineering side: wire the Stripe-Tax-mode alarm (alerting.md sketch) so a regression to exclusive pages a human. If web checkout must open before the flip, geo-block UK/EU checkout rather than sell VAT-exclusive — do not launch UK/EU web billing in the current state.

findings.title: Apple Small Business Program not enrolled — first mobile subscriber locks Suppr into 30% commission for 12 months
findings.detail: GROW-53/ENG-3 is still Todo, priority High, labelled grace-only + launch-blocker, due 2026-06-30 — now 5 days overdue with no progress recorded since 2026-05-13 (apple-sbp-notes.md, carried forward in billing-copy.md §7). SBP has a fiscal-period effective-date lag of up to ~6 weeks after enrolment. No user-facing honesty defect (the user pays Apple's priceString either way — SBP only changes Suppr's net), but per the standing rule 'must be active BEFORE first paid sub or that user locks 30%/12mo', accepting the first paid mobile subscription before SBP is effective is a first-transaction mistake that cannot be undone. Nothing in the repo can close this; it is 100% a founder action in App Store Connect.
findings.recommendation: Grace enrols in SBP (App Store Connect → Agreements, Tax, and Banking → Small Business Program) this week and records the effective date. Do not flip the paid SKU to for-sale (or ramp the mobile paywall to real users) until the SBP effective date is confirmed. Keep this as literal line one of the pre-launch checklist.

findings.title: Zero alerting reaches a human if either payment webhook fails — a broken first transaction would be silent
findings.detail: Both webhook routes correctly capture failures to Sentry (captureRouteError in app/api/stripe/webhook/route.ts:51-58 and app/api/revenuecat/webhook/route.ts:157-161), but Alarm 1 (Sentry issue→email), Alarm 4 (Stripe delivery-failure notification), and Alarm 5 (Vercel error-rate) are all 'Not yet wired' (docs/operations/alerting.md:40,81,93 — verified in the doc directly), and RC's dashboard delivery-failure alert has no confirmed-wired status at all. alerting.md's own bar states these must be Wired before App Store submission. Since no paid subscriber exists, the production webhook path has never fired for real — first-time config errors (RC dashboard webhook not pointed at prod, rotated bearer secret, wrong Stripe endpoint secret) are exactly the failures most likely on transaction one, and today the only discovery mechanism is the customer complaining they paid and didn't get Pro.
findings.recommendation: Wire Alarm 1 (Sentry issue-alert → email) and Alarm 4 (Stripe's native webhook-failure notification) — both are ~5-minute dashboard actions per alerting.md:166-241, no code needed — plus RC's dashboard delivery-failure alert. Then run one end-to-end sandbox purchase on each rail (Stripe test-mode checkout; StoreKit sandbox → RC webhook) and confirm profiles.user_tier flips, before the first real payment.

findings.title: No entitlement reconciliation exists for a permanently missed webhook — and the runbook's 'self-heals on next app launch' claim is false as implemented
findings.detail: founder-safety-net.md:75 claims 'the app re-syncs entitlement state from RC on launch, so a missed webhook self-corrects on the user's next open.' Verified false: the client path it references (syncTierToSupabase, apps/mobile/lib/purchases.ts:314-366) writes via the user's own JWT and is rejected every time by the T2 profiles_tier_column_lockdown trigger (42501) — the code itself catches this as status 'lockdown_expected'. No cron reconciles provider truth to profiles.user_tier (app/api/cron/ contains only supabase-advisor-check; RC's REST API is never called anywhere server-side; /api/stripe/subscription-status is read-only display). So if a webhook is missed beyond the retry window (24h RC / 72h Stripe), a paid user stays free (or a cancelled user stays pro) until Grace manually intervenes — violating the rule that subscription state truth lives server-side, reconciled from the provider. The lockdown itself is correct security posture; the missing piece is server-side reconciliation, not loosening the lockdown.
findings.recommendation: Two actions: (1) today, correct founder-safety-net.md §4b to match §4a's honest manual-replay framing so nobody relies on a control that doesn't exist; (2) ship a scheduled reconciliation cron (new app/api/cron/ route on the existing scheduled-crons.yml) that compares RC GET /subscribers/{app_user_id} + Stripe subscriptions.list against profiles.user_tier for non-free users and writes drift via updateProfileTierServiceRole (which already floor-protects lifetime_pro). Cheap at current scale; needs pagination/rate-limit awareness before a viral spike. File a Linear issue — currently untracked.

findings.title: The purchase surface is unverified by pixels — every billing capture in the evidence set shows the wrong state, so 'disclosure visible before purchase' cannot be signed off
findings.detail: Personally inspected all four captures. (1) mobile/paywall.png is the subscriptionsUnavailable fallback (offerings resolved with zero packages): CTA reads 'Open App Store to subscribe', and by code guard (paywall.tsx:474,1113,1148,1162-1164) the plan selector, prices, AND the paywall-autorenew-disclosure block are all unmounted — the capture proves the one state where disclosure is guaranteed absent, not that it renders. (2) web/settings-billing-desktop.png and -mobile.png are 404 pages ('We couldn't find that page') — captured against /settings/billing; the real route is app/account/billing/page.tsx. (3) mobile/billing.png and mobile/settings.png are the same Free-plan Settings screen (gracehowse+test, 'Free plan', Upgrade CTA) — zero rendered evidence of a Pro subscriber's cancel path. (4) No dark-mode or mobile-viewport pricing capture exists, and web/pricing-desktop.png cuts off above the price/CTA/VAT section. The code-level story is strong (CMA six-element disclosure string, provider-authoritative prices), but per the visual-validation-mandatory rule and this audit's brief, the rendered purchase flow is currently unproven, and release sign-off on the money path cannot be issued on this evidence.
findings.recommendation: Re-capture before first payment: (a) mobile paywall with a working StoreKit/RevenueCat sandbox so offerings resolve and the disclosure block mounts; (b) /account/billing (correct route) desktop + mobile-web, both a Free and a Stripe-Pro account; (c) mobile Settings with a Pro-entitled account to evidence the cancel path; (d) /pricing scrolled to the price/CTA/disclosure section, light + dark. Separately, confirm why offerings returned empty in the capture environment — if that fallback can occur in production it is the paywall real users would see.

findings.title: Full auto-renewal disclosure sits below the fold while the sticky trial CTA is visible from the first frame
findings.detail: Code-confirmed (not pixel-confirmed — see MP-05): the CMA six-element disclosure block (paywall-autorenew-disclosure, paywall.tsx:1164) renders in the scroll content after hero, value grid, comparison matrix, plan selector, and trust strip, while the purchase button is a separately-rendered sticky bar (paywall-sticky-primary-cta, paywall.tsx:1300) pinned bottom from frame one. A user can tap 'Start free trial' within seconds of the paywall opening having seen the 'no payment now' chip (which IS in the sticky bar — good) but never the renewal price / auto-renews-until-cancelled / cancel-path sentence. The disclosure content itself is excellent (provider-authoritative priceString, duration-based trial copy, VAT-inclusive note, refund line) — this is purely a sequencing/placement gap. Mitigation exists: Apple's native purchase sheet shows trial + renewal price at confirm, but a paywall this carefully built to the CMA's six-point test shouldn't rely on Apple's sheet to complete its own disclosure. My rule: renewal terms must be unambiguous on every paywall — that means visible without scrolling on the surface that carries the CTA.
findings.recommendation: Promote a condensed one-line disclosure into the always-visible sticky area under the CTA — e.g. 'Free for 7 days, then {priceString}/yr. Renews until cancelled — cancel in iOS Settings.' Keep the full paragraph in the scroll body. Route the exact copy to legal-reviewer (mandatory for billing surfaces) and the placement to ui-product-designer; mirror the pattern on the web upgrade dialog. Small change, ship behind the standard visual flag with before/after captures per the re-capture pass in MP-05.

findings.title: Meal-plan day-count Pro gate is client-only — save_meal_plan RPC accepts a 7-day plan from a Free user
findings.detail: Verified directly: supabase/migrations/20260503100400_save_meal_plan_rpc.sql validates only day range 1..7 (line 92) and auth.uid(); it never reads profiles.user_tier, and the meal_plan_days/meal_plan_meals RLS policies are identity-only. Both web (src/app/components/MealPlanner.tsx) and mobile (apps/mobile/app/(tabs)/planner.tsx) enforce 'Free = 1 day' purely in UI. Anyone with their own valid JWT can call supabase.rpc('save_meal_plan', …) with a 7-day payload and it persists and renders normally — a pure paywall bypass of the 'Pro unlocks the full week' upgrade trigger (no AI cost, but it hollows out one of the core upgrade moments). This is the third instance of the exact bug class already fixed twice on sibling tables: saves cap (20260426100000) and recipe-publish gate (20260426100100), both moved server-side after the 2026-04-19 sweep called the client-only versions monetisation leaks.
findings.recommendation: Mirror the prior two fixes' shape — one migration adding a tier predicate inside save_meal_plan's day-loop (reject v_day_num > 1 when the caller's tier is free, via the existing auth_profile_user_tier() helper, using the file family's 22023/42501 error convention). Confirm the RPC is the sole INSERT path to meal_plan_days before deciding whether a belt-and-braces RLS WITH CHECK is also worth adding. File a Linear issue (currently untracked). Not first-transaction-breaking, but ship pre-launch while the fix is one migration.

findings.title: No out-of-order/replay-ordering guard on either webhook's tier writes — a stale event can silently re-grant or wrongly revoke entitlement
findings.detail: Neither processStripeWebhookEvent (src/lib/stripe/webhookProcess.ts:61-87,164-171) nor the RC dispatch (src/lib/revenuecat/webhookProcess.ts) compares the incoming event's timestamp against the last event that wrote the user's tier. Concrete risks: Stripe redelivering subscription.updated(active) after deleted re-upgrades a cancelled user (also reproducible via the documented bulk-replay runbook, which replays in list order); a stale RC EXPIRATION landing after a fresh RENEWAL free-tiers a currently-paying user (RC's 26h freshness window bounds this to same-day races only). RC's CANCELLATION/BILLING_ISSUE no-ops are correct-by-construction for the most common race — good. Two minor siblings folded in: the Stripe dedup fails open on non-23505 DB errors while RC's fails closed (undocumented asymmetry — worth a one-line doc note, RC's retry covers it), and TRANSFER's origin-tier fallback read can race an in-flight EXPIRATION (rare path, unguarded).
findings.recommendation: Persist a last-entitlement-event timestamp alongside user_tier (RC already carries event_timestamp_ms; Stripe events carry created) and no-op any tier write whose event is older than the stored value — one additive column plus one comparison in each write path, plus tests. This also makes the manual bulk-replay runbook safe to run in any order. Document the Stripe/RC dedup fail-open/fail-closed asymmetry as intentional in the same PR.

findings.title: Root landing page and in-app upgrade dialog have zero region/VAT wiring — they will not self-correct when Stripe Tax flips, and every consumer price in the product is a GBP literal
findings.detail: app/(landing)/LandingPage.tsx's Pricing() section and src/app/components/suppr/upgrade-paywall-dialog.tsx (hardcoded '£7.99'/'£59.99' fallbacks at lines 171-172) contain no detectRegion call, no VAT note, and no reference to STRIPE_TAX_ENABLED — confirmed by grep. When the MP-01 flag flips, only /pricing starts rendering the VAT-inclusive line; these two surfaces keep showing a bare GBP sticker with no tax disclosure. Broader: every consumer-facing price digit on web is a GBP literal from src/lib/landing/pricingTiers.ts:89,133,135 — a US visitor (the bulk of the MFP-refugee capture cohort, live since 2026-05-03) sees £7.99 with no $ equivalent, a real conversion drag on the priority acquisition moment, and a direct gap against the locked 'pricing must be region-aware' direction. Mobile is exempt and correct: Apple's storefront-localised, VAT-inclusive priceString handles this natively. Untracked in Linear; unlike MP-01 this is engineering-closable today.
findings.recommendation: Wire both surfaces to the same region/VAT plumbing /pricing uses: thread regionCurrency + resolveRenderedVatNote into LandingPage's Pricing() (server component — headers available) and give the in-app dialog a client-side region source (or have it consume a server-provided region prop). Sequence USD/EUR display support in the SSOT as part of the same work (see MP-10) — for the MFP window, USD display is the highest-value currency to add first. File one Linear issue covering both surfaces.

findings.title: Latent display/charge currency mismatch: the day EUR Stripe Prices are configured, EU visitors see £7.99 but get charged a EUR amount with no reconciliation
findings.detail: Traced full call chain: PricingTiersGrid passes regionCurrency to CheckoutButton, which forwards currency:'EUR' to /api/stripe/checkout, whose resolveProStripePriceId returns a EUR-denominated Price the moment STRIPE_PRICE_PRO_{MONTHLY,ANNUAL}_EUR env vars are populated (src/lib/stripe/resolveProStripePrice.ts:35-46). But the card the visitor just read always displays the hardcoded GBP string — PRICING_TIERS has no EUR display fields, and no code path reconciles displayed amount against the charged Price. Dormant today (EUR SKUs unconfigured, so the GBP fallback always wins), but it activates with a single env-var change and no code change — a shown-price ≠ charged-price defect, which is the one billing sin the rest of this codebase has been so careful to avoid. Evidence file's confidence 8/10; the trace is complete and I found no counter-path.
findings.recommendation: Correct fix: add per-currency display fields to the pricing SSOT and render the amount for the resolved regionCurrency, so display and Stripe Price come from one reconciled source before EUR SKUs are ever configured. Defensive backstop in the same PR: a startup/env assertion (verify-production-env is the natural home) that fails loudly if EUR Price env vars are set while the display layer has no EUR prices. File a Linear issue so the env-var flip can't happen ahead of the display fix.

findings.title: Confirmed-correct money-path foundations — do not re-flag these
findings.detail: For the aggregator: the following were verified solid across the four evidence files and my spot-checks, and should not be re-derived or re-flagged. (1) Stripe signature verification via constructEvent on the raw body; RC constant-time bearer compare + 26h freshness window (the strongest auth RC supports). (2) DB-backed PK idempotency on both webhooks (stripe_webhook_events, revenuecat_events) — the 'double-applied webhook' first-transaction risk named in this audit's brief is closed. (3) Four-layer profiles.user_tier lockdown (UPDATE trigger, forward-compat column list, INSERT trigger closing the GDPR delete-reinsert hole, anon RPC EXECUTE revocation) — client self-grant is blocked; all 10 AI/Pro server routes independently re-verify tier via the service-role getUserTier that fails closed. (4) lifetime_pro floor protection shared by both provider write paths. (5) Provider-authoritative pricing everywhere it matters: Apple priceString on the mobile paywall (storefront-local, VAT-inclusive by construction), Stripe priceAmount on the web subscription card, no hardcoded renewal numbers in disclosure copy. (6) Honest cancellation: no retention save-wall, cancel button equal-or-greater weight, IAP branch never fakes a cancel control it can't honour, every billingPortalDecision branch lands somewhere actionable. (7) Refund copy consistent across all three surfaces (7-day voluntary + UK/EU 14-day statutory correctly layered), single legal SSOT, pinned by tests. This is a genuinely well-built money path at the code level — the blockers in this audit are configuration, enrolment, alerting, and evidence, not engineering quality.
findings.recommendation: No action. Reference this finding to suppress duplicate re-filing of settled items in aggregation.

findings.title: First UK/EU web sale will be VAT-exclusive with no VAT collected — STRIPE_TAX_ENABLED is off and the flip checklist is fully unactioned
findings.detail: The load-bearing VAT posture (docs/decisions/2026-04-19-consumer-vat-posture-uk-eu.md) requires UK/EU consumer prices to be VAT-inclusive from £1/€1 via Stripe Tax in inclusive mode. At main HEAD, STRIPE_TAX_ENABLED=false (.env.example:103), all four items of TODO.md's ship-blocking 'Flip STRIPE_TAX_ENABLED=true' checklist are unchecked (Stripe dashboard activation, tax_behavior on the 4 Price objects, Vercel flag, live UK smoke test), and docs/operations/stripe-tax-launch-checklist.md's last status (2026-05-13) is 'dashboard config + Vercel flag pending'. With the flag off: /pricing shows 'Price excludes any applicable taxes' to every visitor including UK/EU, resolveRenderedVatNote suppresses the VAT note, and /api/stripe/checkout omits automatic_tax entirely — so the first real UK web subscriber is charged £7.99 with zero VAT collected, and Suppr owes the VAT retroactively out of net revenue. The interim copy is honest (deliberately engineered so the inclusive claim can never render ahead of Stripe computing VAT — good), but honesty of copy does not close the accruing liability, and there is no engineering-visible alarm or expiry on the unflipped flag. This is the canonical 'wrong VAT posture locked in with the first payment' case the audit was scoped to catch.
findings.recommendation: Before accepting the first real web payment: Grace completes the four dashboard actions in the TODO.md checklist (activate Stripe Tax in inclusive mode, set tax_behavior=inclusive on the 4 Price objects, flip STRIPE_TAX_ENABLED=true in Vercel + redeploy, run one live UK checkout smoke test and confirm the 'includes VAT' note renders on /pricing). Verify the live Vercel flag value directly — this audit only inferred it from repo artifacts. Track as a launch-blocker Linear issue so the forcing function is stronger than a TODO checkbox. Owner: Grace (dashboard) + monetisation-architect (verification).

findings.title: Apple Small Business Program not enrolled — the first real mobile paid subscriber locks Suppr into 30% commission for 12 months
findings.detail: GROW-53/ENG-3 (Todo, High, grace-only + launch-blocker) was due 2026-06-30 and is now 5 days overdue with no progress recorded since 2026-05-13. SBP must be active BEFORE the first paid subscription or that subscriber's revenue is locked at 30%/12mo instead of 15%; enrolment also carries an effective-date lag of up to ~6 weeks, so every day of delay pushes out the earliest safe date to flip the paid SKU live. No user-facing honesty defect (the user pays Apple's priceString either way — verified in the paywall disclosure builder), but this is the mobile-side twin of LEGAL-001: a mistake that gets permanently locked in by the first real transaction and cannot be fixed retroactively. 100% founder-gated in App Store Connect; no code in this repo can close it.
findings.recommendation: Grace enrols in the Small Business Program (App Store Connect → Agreements, Tax, and Banking) immediately, confirms the effective date, and the mobile paid SKU does not go on sale before that effective date. Keep GROW-53/ENG-3 as the first line item in every pre-launch checklist review until enrolment + effective date are confirmed. Owner: Grace.

findings.title: The purchase-consent surfaces are visually unverified — every billing screenshot in the evidence set is a 404, a fallback state, or the wrong account
findings.detail: Verified pixel-by-pixel this pass: (a) web/settings-billing-desktop.png and -mobile.png render the app's 404 page ('We couldn't find that page') — captured against /settings/billing, which does not exist; the real route is app/account/billing/page.tsx. (b) mobile/paywall.png was captured in the subscriptionsUnavailable fallback (offeringsReady && packages.length === 0, paywall.tsx:474): CTA reads 'Open App Store to subscribe', and the plan selector, prices, and the entire auto-renewal disclosure block (paywall-autorenew-disclosure) are conditionally unmounted — the screenshot proves the one state where the disclosure is guaranteed NOT to render. Note the fallback still asserts 'Try Pro free for 7 days… Cancel anytime in iOS Settings' with no price on screen. (c) mobile/billing.png and mobile/settings.png are the same Free-plan settings screen (gracehowse+test, 'Sloe Pro — Upgrade'), so there is zero rendered evidence of what a paying subscriber sees when trying to cancel. (d) No pricing dark-mode or mobile-viewport capture exists, and web/pricing-desktop.png cuts off above the price/CTA section. The disclosure copy in code is genuinely strong (CMA six-element string, provider-authoritative prices, 26 passing copy tests), but a legal reviewer cannot sign off a consent surface for the first real payment from code alone — the rendered paywall and the web billing/cancel page are currently unverified, not verified-and-passing.
findings.recommendation: Re-run the capture pass before the first real payment: web billing at /account/billing (authenticated, with an active Stripe test subscription), mobile paywall with a working RevenueCat/StoreKit sandbox so prices + the auto-renewal disclosure render, mobile settings on a Pro-entitled account showing the manage/cancel path, and /pricing scrolled to the price/CTA section in light + dark. Legal sign-off on the paywall and cancel path is withheld until those pixels exist. Owner: executor (capture) → legal-reviewer (re-review).

findings.title: Both money-path webhooks fail silently — Sentry captures exist but no alarm reaches a human
findings.detail: Every failure path in both webhook handlers is captured (captureRouteError on Stripe route.ts:51-58 and RC route.ts:157-161; RC additionally captures auth failures and stale-event rejections), but per docs/operations/alerting.md: Alarm 1 (Sentry issue → email) is 'Not yet wired' (line 40), Alarm 4 (Stripe delivery-failure dashboard notification) is 'Not yet wired' (line 81), RC has no equivalent alarm line item at all, and Alarm 5 (Vercel error-rate) is also unwired. If either handler starts failing 100% on launch day — bad deploy, rotated secret, Supabase outage — Stripe/RC retry for 72h/24h, Sentry silently accumulates, and discovery depends on Grace proactively checking dashboards or a paying user complaining that their purchase didn't unlock Pro. Webhook misconfiguration is exactly the class of failure that first manifests on the first real (never-before-exercised) live transaction, and both fixes are ~5-minute dashboard actions per the runbook's own instructions.
findings.recommendation: Before the first real payment: wire Alarm 1 (Sentry issue-alert rule → email, alerting.md:172-186) — this immediately turns every existing captureRouteError call into a page — and enable Stripe's native Alarm 4 delivery-failure notification. Add the RC dashboard's delivery-failure alert as a named line item in alerting.md rather than a 'mirror the Stripe procedure' aside. Owner: Grace (dashboards), doc update: executor.

findings.title: No entitlement reconciliation exists if a webhook is permanently missed — and founder-safety-net.md's 'self-heals on next app launch' claim is false as implemented
findings.detail: docs/operations/founder-safety-net.md §4b claims a missed RevenueCat webhook 'self-corrects on the user's next open'. The path it references (syncTierToSupabase, apps/mobile/lib/purchases.ts:319-370) writes user_tier via the client's own JWT and is hard-rejected (42501) by the T2 tier-column lockdown trigger (20260503100000_profiles_tier_column_lockdown.sql:39-45) — every time, for every user, by design; the code itself catches this as 'lockdown_expected'. There is no cron reconciling provider truth to profiles.user_tier (RC's REST subscriber API is never called anywhere; /api/stripe/subscription-status is read-only and never writes tier), so a paying user whose webhook is missed stays Free (or a canceled user stays Pro) until Grace manually notices and fixes it by hand — the exact 'Pro never / Pro forever' bug class the webhook was built to close. The lockdown itself is the RIGHT security posture (do not loosen it); the gap is the absent server-side reconciliation plus the doc making a safety claim that isn't true. Combined with LEGAL-004 (silent failure), a first-purchase webhook failure would strand a paying customer without Pro and without anyone knowing.
findings.recommendation: Two parts: (1) immediately correct founder-safety-net.md §4b to the honest manual-replay-only framing (matching §4a's accurate Stripe section) so nobody relies on a control that doesn't exist — effort minutes; (2) ship a scheduled reconciliation cron (new app/api/cron/ route on the existing scheduled-crons.yml) that compares RC REST + Stripe subscription truth against profiles.user_tier for non-free users and writes drift via updateProfileTierServiceRole (which already carries the lifetime_pro floor). Cheap at current N=1 scale; needs pagination before a viral spike. Owner: executor; Linear issue required (currently untracked).

findings.title: Full auto-renewal disclosure sits below the fold while the sticky 'Start free trial' CTA is visible from the first frame (mobile paywall)
findings.detail: The disclosure string itself is excellent — it maps to the UK CMA's six required elements (renewal price from Apple's live priceString, frequency, auto-renew-until-cancelled, cancel path, trial length, first-charge timing) and matches the 2026-04-19 renewal-disclosure rewrite this reviewer is bound to enforce. But the disclosure block (paywall-autorenew-disclosure) renders in the scroll content after the hero, value grid, comparison matrix, plan selector, and trust strip, while the primary purchase CTA is a separately-rendered sticky bar pinned to the bottom at all scroll positions. A user can tap Subscribe within seconds of the paywall opening having seen only the 'no payment now' chip (which does render in the sticky bar — a real mitigation) but not the renewal price / auto-renew / cancel-path sentence. This is sequencing drift from the spirit of the renewal-disclosure decision, not absence of disclosure. Confirmed from code scroll order only — the rendered capture was the fallback state (LEGAL-003), so pixel confirmation is pending.
findings.recommendation: Promote a condensed one-line disclosure into the always-visible sticky area alongside the no-payment chip — e.g. 'Then {priceString}/yr, auto-renews, cancel anytime' — keeping the full six-element paragraph in the scroll content. Ship on web's checkout entry point in the same commit per the parity rule. Treat as a product decision to make deliberately, not assume away, given how carefully the rest of this surface was built to the CMA letter. Owner: ui-product-designer + monetisation-architect; re-verify from pixels after LEGAL-003's re-capture.

findings.title: No out-of-order/replay-ordering guard on either webhook's tier writes — a stale event can silently re-grant or wrongly revoke a paid entitlement
findings.detail: Stripe: processStripeWebhookEvent has no event.created comparison, so a redelivered customer.subscription.updated(active) arriving after deleted re-upgrades a canceled user with no subscription behind it; the documented bulk-replay runbook procedure would trigger exactly this during a manual replay. RevenueCat: CANCELLATION/BILLING_ISSUE are correctly no-ops (good), but EXPIRATION/REFUND/SUBSCRIPTION_PAUSED unconditionally downgrade with no supersession check — a stale same-day EXPIRATION landing after a fresh RENEWAL free-tiers a currently-paying user (26h staleness check bounds but does not eliminate this). TRANSFER's origin-tier fallback read can race an in-flight EXPIRATION. For the first paying cohort this means a paid user can be silently downgraded — a 'surprise loss of paid service' trust breach, the mirror image of a surprise charge.
findings.recommendation: Persist a last-event-timestamp column alongside user_tier (Stripe: event.created; RC: event_timestamp_ms, already in every payload) and no-op any tier write from an event older than the one that last wrote that user's tier — small additive change in applyTierForSubscription, the deleted branch, and RC's grant + expiration paths. Also add the one-line doc note on the Stripe-fails-open vs RC-fails-closed dedup asymmetry so a future edit to one doesn't assume the other's behaviour. Owner: executor; Linear issue required.

findings.title: Root landing page '/' and the in-app upgrade dialog have zero region/VAT wiring — they will not self-correct when STRIPE_TAX_ENABLED flips
findings.detail: LandingPage.tsx's Pricing() section and upgrade-paywall-dialog.tsx render hardcoded GBP SSOT prices with no detectRegion call, no resolveRenderedVatNote, and no VAT disclosure on any flag state (confirmed by grep — neither symbol appears in either file). Only /pricing is wired to the flag, so after LEGAL-001 is fixed, these two surfaces will still show a bare '£7.99' with no 'includes VAT' note. In inclusive mode the displayed number remains the amount charged, so this is a disclosure/consistency gap rather than a price surprise — but it leaves two conversion surfaces diverging in commitment from /pricing, violating the cross-surface consistency rule, and the upgrade dialog is the surface an in-app user actually converts through. Closable by engineering today; currently untracked in Linear or TODO.md.
findings.recommendation: Wire both surfaces to the same region/VAT note plumbing as /pricing (the dialog needs a client-side region source — thread it from the server or an API rather than duplicating detectRegion logic). Sequence with the LEGAL-001 flag flip so all three web pricing surfaces say the same thing on day one. Owner: executor; open a Linear issue.

findings.title: Dormant landmine: the day EUR Stripe SKUs are configured, EU visitors will see '£7.99' but be charged a EUR amount with no reconciliation
findings.detail: PricingTiersGrid always displays the hardcoded GBP SSOT amount, while CheckoutButton forwards currency:'EUR' to /api/stripe/checkout, whose resolveProStripePriceId returns a EUR-denominated Stripe Price the moment STRIPE_PRICE_PRO_MONTHLY_EUR/ANNUAL_EUR env vars are populated. PRICING_TIERS has no priceEur field — the EUR amount lives only in the Stripe Price object, invisible to the React tree, and no code path reconciles displayed £ against charged €. Not live today (EUR SKUs unconfigured, resolver falls back to GBP — traced through the full call chain), but it activates with a single env-var change and no accompanying display fix. Displayed-price-differs-from-charged-price is a per-se misleading-pricing violation the day it fires; treat ambiguous billing as P0 at activation time.
findings.recommendation: Before ever populating the EUR env vars: either add priceEur/annualPriceEur to the SSOT and make PricingTiersGrid render the region-matched amount, or add a hard guard in the checkout route refusing a EUR session when the display layer can't render EUR (fail to GBP display + GBP charge, consistently). Add a comment on the EUR env-var lines in .env.example pointing at this dependency so the env change can't ship alone. Owner: monetisation-architect + executor; Linear issue required.

findings.title: Meal-plan day-count Pro gate is client-only — save_meal_plan RPC accepts a 7-day plan from a Free-tier user
findings.detail: The Free=1-day / Base+Pro=3-7-day plan gate is enforced only in UI on both platforms (planner.tsx clamps + 'Upgrade required' alert; MealPlanner.tsx identical). The save_meal_plan RPC validates day range 1-7 and auth.uid() but never reads profiles.user_tier, and the meal_plan_days/meal_plan_meals RLS policies are identity-only — so a Free user with their own JWT can rpc('save_meal_plan') a 7-day plan directly and every read path renders it, fully bypassing the 'Pro unlocks the full week' upsell. This is the third instance of a bug class already found and fixed twice (saves cap 20260426100000; publish gate 20260426100100). Pure entitlement/monetisation leak — no AI cost, no consumer harm, no billing-honesty issue — but it undermines the paywall's core value proposition on the Plan tab. Evidence confidence 9/10 (RPC body read directly).
findings.recommendation: Mirror the two prior fixes' shape: add a tier predicate inside save_meal_plan (read via the existing auth_profile_user_tier() helper; raise on v_day_num > 1 when tier='free'), one migration, consistent with the file family's 22023/42501 error-code convention. Confirm the RPC is the sole INSERT path before deciding whether a belt-and-braces RLS WITH CHECK is worth a second layer. Owner: executor; Linear issue required (evidence file says untracked).

findings.title: Weight-loss projection card ('70.7 kg in ~5 weeks', downward trend line) renders on the paywall purchase surface — new finding from pixels, not in any evidence file
findings.detail: mobile/paywall.png shows, directly under the 'Try Pro free for 7 days' headline, a 'PROJECTED WEIGHT' card: '70.7 kg in ~5 weeks' with a downward dashed trend line, 'If you keep your current pace — last 7 days averaged 1,556 kcal/day', and 'Based on 7,700 kcal ≈ 1 kg. An estimate, not a promise.' The hedging is genuinely good — user's own data, pace-conditional framing, visible heuristic, explicit not-a-promise disclaimer — and it carefully avoids claiming Pro CAUSES the loss. But it juxtaposes a body-weight outcome projection with the subscription CTA on a conversion surface, which brushes against the standing posture (no outcome claims as purchase motivators; avoid transformation framing) and the diversity-inclusion guard rail on body/weight language. It also raises a calm-mode question: calm_mode hides aim numbers product-wide (ENG-1098) — does the paywall respect it, or does a calm-mode user get a weight projection at the exact moment of purchase pressure? Note this card renders even in the degraded offerings-empty state captured here.
findings.recommendation: Do not remove reflexively — the hedged, own-data framing is defensible and likely converts honestly. Required before sign-off: (1) route to diversity-inclusion per the standing guard rail for body/weight language on a pressure surface; (2) verify the paywall suppresses this card for calm_mode users (and for users with no weight goal); (3) confirm the projection never renders on marketing/logged-out surfaces where it would become an outcome claim rather than a personal estimate. Owner: nutrition-engine (framing) + diversity-inclusion (review) + executor (calm-mode gate).

findings.title: 'Unlimited imports' marketed as a Pro perk on /pricing while recipe import is free-by-design for all tiers — the true differential is unlimited SAVES
findings.detail: web/pricing-desktop.png shows a 4-card grid under the 'SLOE PRO' hero whose first card reads 'Unlimited imports — Save any recipe from a link or Reel.' Per the entitlements census, app/api/recipe-import/route.ts has no tier check — import is the intentionally free viral wedge, unlimited for everyone. The real Free-tier constraint is the save cap (FREE_SAVE_LIMIT; 'Unlimited saved recipes' is correctly the Base/Pro SSOT feature at pricingTiers.ts:153, and the SSOT's own audit comments show deliberate care about 'unlimited' claims elsewhere — e.g. refusing 'unlimited AI logging' because of the 100/day cap). Framing 'unlimited imports' as the Pro perk mislabels a free capability as paid — mild overstatement risk in the direction of overselling Pro, and inconsistent with the SSOT's otherwise disciplined subtractive-audit posture. Medium confidence: the comparison table lower on the page may clarify per-tier limits, and the card body ('Save any recipe') hints the intended claim is saves, not imports.
findings.recommendation: Rename the card to match the true differential — 'Unlimited saves — keep every recipe you import from a link or Reel' — or move the unlimited claim onto saves explicitly, keeping it consistent with pricingTiers.ts's careful language. Verify the mobile paywall's comparison component (shared SSOT) doesn't carry the same framing. Owner: ui-product-designer + monetisation-architect.

findings.title: Unverified: does Stripe Checkout collect express consent to immediate performance (EU/UK 14-day withdrawal waiver) as the Terms claim?
findings.detail: app/terms/page.tsx states that starting the subscription immediately requires the user's express consent to lose the 14-day withdrawal right once performance begins — the correct legal pattern — but the billing-copy audit explicitly did NOT trace the Stripe Checkout session creation to confirm a consent checkbox/acknowledgment (e.g. Stripe's consent_collection or custom_text) is actually configured at that step. If it isn't, the Terms assert a consent checkpoint the flow doesn't implement: the first EU/UK web subscriber would retain the full 14-day withdrawal right (consumer harm nil — the terms already layer the 14-day statutory right on top of the voluntary 7-day policy — but the commitments mismatch between Terms and flow is exactly the web-vs-flow divergence this review exists to catch). Mobile is exempt (Apple owns IAP consent). Existence of the gap is unverified — this is a verification task, not a confirmed defect.
findings.recommendation: Trace the checkout session creation in app/api/stripe/checkout/route.ts and the Stripe dashboard config for consent_collection/custom_text; if absent, add Stripe Checkout's consent/acknowledgment line ('I agree that access starts immediately and I lose my 14-day withdrawal right, retaining the refund policy above') before the first EU/UK sale. Fold into the LEGAL-001 UK checkout smoke test so both are verified in one pass. Owner: monetisation-architect.
